title: Gap analysis vs prior plan (@explore subagent)

data.text:
There is a prior agency plan at /home/uzer/.kilo/plans/1783597303132-marketing-hub-agency-plan.md (read it). It describes a 3-phase, 5-sprint roadmap adding ~16 tables to a Next.js Marketing Hub (proposals, contracts, client portal, onboarding, projects, retainers, rate cards, capacity, booking, approvals, client reports, reputation, AI proposal generator).

Your job: determine what of that PLAN is already implemented in the actual codebase vs still missing. Search the real code at /home/uzer/marketing-hub and /home/uzer/marketing-hub-public.

For each planned item below, report IMPLEMENTED / PARTIAL / MISSING with evidence (file paths/routes/tables you found):
- 1.1 White-label client portal (`/c/[token]`, `portal_links.client_id`)
- 1.2 Proposals + e-signature (`proposals`, `proposal_signatures` tables; `/finance/proposals`)
- 1.3 Contracts (`contracts` table; `/finance/contracts`)
- 1.4 Client onboarding (`onboarding_workflows`, `onboarding_runs`)
- 1.5 Client messaging (`client_messages`)
- 1.6 Automated client reporting (`client_reports`, PDF)
- 2.1 Projects/milestones/files (`projects`, `milestones`, `project_files`; `tasks.project_id`)
- 2.2 Retainer management (`retainer_usage`)
- 2.3 Rate cards (`rate_cards`)
- 2.4 Capacity planning (`capacity_allocations`)
- 2.5 Deliverable approvals (`deliverable_approvals`)
- 3.1 AI proposal generator
- 3.2 Public booking/intake (`booking_pages`)
- 3.3 Reputation management (`reviews`, `testimonials`)

Also verify: does `portal_links` have `client_id` or only `client_name`? Does `tasks` have `project_id`? What is the Sidebar currently showing (read components/Sidebar.tsx)?

Also briefly check /home/uzer/empire/marketing-hub (Tauri shell) and /home/uzer/empire/midnight-terminal: note their role and current status (build blocked? tokens package?). And check whether a Capacitor/mobile path exists anywhere.

Report a clear gap table (Planned Item | Status | Evidence) plus a short paragraph on overall product maturity. Do NOT edit files — research only.

<content>
1: # Marketing Hub — Agency Completeness Plan
2: 
3: **Goal:** Ship every feature a professional marketing agency needs to run client work, bill accurately, and deliver a premium client experience — all inside this Next.js + SQLite hub.
4: 
5: **Architecture decision:** Client-facing portal and public intake pages stay in `marketing-hub` as public token-scoped routes. No cross-app dependency. Private workspace and public client surface share one repo, one database, one deploy. This keeps rollout atomic and avoids sync/queue complexity between `marketing-hub` and `marketing-hub-public`.
6: 
7: **Current state:**
8: - Core marketing execution is solid: SEO, ads, CRM, campaigns, email, landing pages, funnels, analytics, automations, A/B tests, inbox, surveys, reports, copy generator.
9: - Agency/finance layer is partially built: **timesheets**, **client invoices**, **project PnL**, **contractors**, **payouts**, **billing**, **portal links**, **agency client accounts**, **approvals**.
10: - Existing `clients` table (id, workspace, name, industry, website, contact_email, notes) is the canonical agency client directory. `timesheets`, `client_invoices`, and `project_pnl` already reference `client_id`.
11: - `tasks` exists but `project` is currently a TEXT string, not a FK. Add `project_id TEXT` to `tasks` so tasks can link to the new `projects` table.
12: - `/c/[token]` route and public booking pages do not exist yet; only `portal_links` token-generation API exists.
13: - What’s missing is the **client-facing experience layer** and **agency operations depth** that top platforms ship.
14: 
15: **Reuse rule:** Do not create a second client directory. All new agency tables (`proposals`, `contracts`, `projects`, `retainer_usage`, `deliverable_approvals`, `client_messages`, `client_reports`) must link to the existing `clients.id`. `agency_client_accounts` is separate and stays separate; it is for platform/sub-account billing, not agency client records.
16: 
17: **Workspace scoping:** Existing tables mix `workspace TEXT` and `workspace_id TEXT`. New tables must use `workspace_id TEXT` with FK to `workspaces.id`.
18: 
19: **Pattern to reuse everywhere:**
20: - Data: `lib/db/index.ts` auto-migration `CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS` inside `init()`.
21: - API: `app/api/<module>/route.ts` with GET (list), GET `[id]`, POST (create), PATCH `[id]`, DELETE `[id]`. Use existing `json/err` helpers.
22: - Pages: `app/<module>/page.tsx` and `app/<module>/[id]/page.tsx` with `"use client"`, `useFetch`, `Modal`, `apiFetch`, `lucide-react` icons, card/section layout.
23: - Nav: `components/Sidebar.tsx` group entries by department.
24: 
25: ---
26: 
27: ## Phase 1: Client Experience Layer
28: 
29: ### 1.1 White-Label Client Portal
30: **Files to create/modify:**
31: - `app/c/[token]/page.tsx` — token-scoped dashboard: project status, invoices, files, messages.
32: - `app/api/portal/[token]/route.ts` — validate token, resolve client from `portal_links.client_name` or explicit link-to-client mapping, return scoped data.
33: - `app/api/portal/[token]/messages/route.ts` — list + create messages for that client.
34: - `app/settings/page.tsx` — add white-label section (logo upload, primary color, sender name).
35: - `lib/db/index.ts` — ensure `portal_links.scope` enforcement logic exists in API routes.
36: 
37: **Data flow:** Token → `portal_links` → resolve to agency contact/client → filter all queries by `clients.id`. The `portal_links` table already has `client_name` but not `client_id`; add `client_id TEXT` to `portal_links` so the API can do strict FK-scoped queries instead of name-matching.
38: 
39: **Acceptance:** A client with a valid portal link sees only their own projects/invoices/messages. Branding reads from workspace settings.
40: 
41: ### 1.2 Proposals + E-Signature
42: **Files to create:**
43: - `lib/db/index.ts` — add `proposals` and `proposal_signatures` tables.
44: - `app/api/finance/proposals/route.ts` — list + create.
45: - `app/api/finance/proposals/[id]/route.ts` — read + update + delete.
46: - `app/api/finance/proposals/[id]/send/route.ts` — generate shareable token, mark sent.
47: - `app/api/finance/proposals/[id]/sign/route.ts` — accept signature payload, mark accepted.
48: - `app/finance/proposals/page.tsx` — list view with status badges, new button.
49: - `app/finance/proposals/[id]/page.tsx` — preview, edit, send, sign flow.
50: 
51: **Data model:**
52: - `proposals`: id, workspace, client_id (FK to clients.id), title, line_items JSON, total, currency, status, due_date, terms, cover_letter, token, viewed_at, accepted_at, created_at.
53: - `proposal_signatures`: id, proposal_id, signer_name, signer_email, signed_at, ip, method.
54: 
55: **Status flow:** draft → sent → viewed → accepted → expired. Default templates: SEO retainer, full-service monthly, one-time project, social media management, PPC management.
56: 
57: ### 1.3 Contracts
58: **Files to create:**
59: - `lib/db/index.ts` — add `contracts` table.
60: - `app/api/finance/contracts/route.ts` — list + create.
61: - `app/api/finance/contracts/[id]/route.ts` — read + update.
62: - `app/api/finance/contracts/[id]/sign/route.ts` — e-signature capture.
63: - `app/finance/contracts/page.tsx` — list with link to proposal.
64: - `app/finance/contracts/[id]/page.tsx` — preview + sign.
65: 
66: **E-signature model:** Support both fast acceptance and formal signing.
67: - `proposal_signatures`: id, proposal_id, signer_name, signer_email, signed_at, ip, method, signature_data JSON (optional).
68: - `contracts`: id, workspace_id, proposal_id, client_id, title, body, status, start_date, end_date, auto_renew, signed_at, signature_data JSON, fee, currency, created_at.
69: - Method enum: `click_accept` vs `canvas_signature`.
70: - `signature_data` stores: for click_accept → `{ "method": "click_accept", "name": "...", "email": "...", "ip": "...", "timestamp": "..." }`; for canvas_signature → `{ "method": "canvas_signature", "image": "data:image/svg+xml;base64,...", "name": "...", "email": "...", "ip": "...", "timestamp": "..." }`.
71: - Proposals default to click_accept. Contracts default to canvas_signature but allow both. This gives a clear UX threshold: proposal = fast, contract = formal.
72: 
73: **UI:**
74: - Proposal sign page: checkbox “I agree to these terms” + typed name + email + submit. No drawing.
75: - Contract sign page: name + email + canvas draw pad + submit. Also allow click_accept fallback if client prefers.
76: - Store acceptance data in `signature_data` JSON on both `proposal_signatures` and `contracts`.
77: 
78: ### 1.4 Client Onboarding
79: **Files to create:**
80: - `lib/db/index.ts` — add `onboarding_workflows`, `onboarding_runs`.
81: - `app/api/onboarding/route.ts` — list + create workflow.
82: - `app/api/onboarding/[id]/route.ts` — read + update step.
83: - `app/api/onboarding/runs/route.ts` — list + create run.
84: - `app/api/onboarding/runs/[id]/route.ts` — advance/complete.
85: - `app/clients/onboarding/page.tsx` — workflow templates.
86: - `app/clients/onboarding/[id]/page.tsx` — run progress.
87: 
88: **Trigger:** contract signed OR manual client add. Step types: email, questionnaire, file_upload, calendar, deliverable.
89: 
90: ### 1.5 Client Messaging
91: **Files to create:**
92: - `lib/db/index.ts` — add `client_messages`.
93: - `app/api/messages/route.ts` — list + create.
94: - `app/clients/messages/page.tsx` — threaded inbox.
95: - `app/c/[token]/messages/page.tsx` — client-side thread.
96: 
97: ### 1.6 Automated Client Reporting
98: **Files to create:**
99: - `lib/db/index.ts` — add `client_reports`.
100: - `app/api/reports/clients/route.ts` — list + create schedule.
101: - `app/api/reports/clients/[id]/route.ts` — read + update + trigger send.
102: - `app/api/reports/clients/[id]/pdf/route.ts` — generate branded PDF via jspdf.
103: - `app/reports/clients/page.tsx` — scheduled reports list.
104: - `app/reports/clients/[id]/page.tsx` — preview + schedule settings.
105: 
106: ---

108: ## Phase 2: Agency Operations Layer
109: 
110: ### 2.1 Project Management
111: **Files to create:**
112: - `lib/db/index.ts` — add `projects`, `milestones`, `project_files`. Extend `tasks` with `project_id TEXT` and `client_id TEXT`. Add `project_id` to `timesheets` if not already present.
113: - `app/api/projects/route.ts` — list + create.
114: - `app/api/projects/[id]/route.ts` — read + update + delete.
115: - `app/api/projects/[id]/files/route.ts` — upload/list/delete.
116: - `app/projects/page.tsx` — kanban/list toggle.
117: - `app/projects/[id]/page.tsx` — detail: overview, files, milestones, linked timesheets/invoices/tasks.
118: 
119: **Fields:**
120: - projects: id, workspace_id, client_id (FK to clients.id), client_name, name, description, status, start_date, end_date, budget, budget_currency, manager_id, created_at.
121: - milestones: id, project_id, name, due_date, status, created_at.
122: - project_files: id, project_id, name, url, size, uploaded_by, created_at.
123: - tasks: add `project_id TEXT`, `client_id TEXT`. Existing `project TEXT` remains for backward compatibility but `project_id` is the canonical link.
124: 
125: **Migration notes:** Auto-migration in `init()` adds the new columns to `tasks` with `ALTER TABLE ADD COLUMN IF NOT EXISTS`. Backfill `client_id` on tasks from existing `client_invoices`/`timesheets` data where possible. `timesheets` already has `project_id` and `client_id` — no migration needed there.
126: 
127: **Integration:** Link tasks, timesheets, invoices, expenses, and campaigns to projects via `project_id` FK.

132: - `app/api/finance/retainers/route.ts` — list + create.

162: - `app/api/approvals/deliverables/route.ts` — list + create.

172: ## Phase 3: Marketing-First Enhancements
173: 
174: ### 3.1 AI Proposal Generator
175: - Add a "Generate with AI" button in `app/finance/proposals/[id]/page.tsx`.
176: - Call existing `/api/copy` or new `/api/finance/proposals/[id]/generate` that drafts `cover_letter` + `line_items` from `campaign_briefs` + `pricing_strategies`.

178: ### 3.2 Public Booking / Intake
179: **Files to create:**
180: - `lib/db/index.ts` — add `booking_pages`.
181: - `app/api/booking/route.ts` — list + create.

189: ### 3.3 Reputation Management

191: - `app/reviews/page.tsx` — pull from `testimonials`, show external review links.

196: ## Navigation / Sidebar Changes

198: 
199: **Revenue group:**
200: - `/finance/proposals` — Proposals
201: - `/finance/contracts` — Contracts
202: - `/finance/retainers` — Retainers
203: - `/finance/rate-cards` — Rate Cards
204: - `/finance/capacity` — Capacity
205: 
206: **Operations group:**
207: - `/projects` — Projects
208: 
209: **Client Success group:**
210: - `/clients/onboarding` — Onboarding
211: - `/clients/messages` — Messages
212: - `/reviews` — Reputation
213: 
214: Keep `/billing` as platform subscription routes; never mix with client invoices.
215: 
216: ---
217: 
218: ## Data Model Summary (Tables to Add)
219: | Table | Purpose |
220: |---|---|
221: | `proposals` | Quote-to-cash proposals with line items |
222: | `proposal_signatures` | E-signatures on proposals |
223: | `contracts` | Contracts linked to proposals/clients |
224: | `onboarding_workflows` | Reusable onboarding step templates |
225: | `onboarding_runs` | Per-client onboarding execution |
226: | `client_messages` | Threaded client ↔ agency messaging |
227: | `client_reports` | Scheduled per-client branded report configs |
228: | `projects` | Projects linked to clients, tasks, invoices |
229: | `milestones` | Project milestones |
230: | `project_files` | Per-project shared documents |
231: | `retainer_usage` | Retainer hour tracking per period |
232: | `rate_cards` | Billable rates per role |
233: | `capacity_allocations` | Team capacity / utilization planning |
234: | `booking_pages` | Public intake/booking pages |
235: | `deliverable_approvals` | Client proofing & approval workflow |
236: 
237: ---
238: 
239: ## Implementation Sequence
240: 1. **Sprint 1:** Proposals + E-signature + Contracts. Unlock quote-to-cash.
241: 2. **Sprint 2:** Client Portal upgrade + Client Messaging. Premium experience.
242: 3. **Sprint 3:** Projects + Milestones + Files + Retainer + Rate Cards. Operations backbone.
243: 4. **Sprint 4:** Client Onboarding + Automated Client Reporting + Capacity Planning. Automation layer.
244: 5. **Sprint 5:** Booking/Intake + Reputation + AI Proposal Generator + polish.
245: 
246: Every sprint must end with all new API routes returning 200 and new sidebar links resolving to 200.
247: 
248: ---
249: 
250: ## Validation
251: - `npm run dev` starts without error.
252: - Every new page returns 200.
253: - Every new module has full CRUD API contract: GET list, GET [id], POST create, PATCH [id], DELETE [id].
254: - Portal token is non-guessable, scoped to one client, and expires.
255: - Proposal click_accept flow: submit → status becomes accepted → draft contract auto-created.
256: - Contract canvas_signature flow: draw + submit → signature_data contains SVG/base64 image, status becomes signed.
257: - Contract click_accept fallback: submit without drawing → signature_data contains click_accept payload, status becomes signed.
258: - Proposal → accepted → contract draft → signed → onboarding triggered (if configured).
259: - Timesheet auto-fills rate from rate card when role selected.
260: - Retainer shows usage alert at 80% and 100%.
261: - Client report PDF renders and downloads.
262: 
263: ---
264: 
265: ## Out of Scope
266: - Switching to separate public app or shared monorepo deploy.
267: - External payment processor integration beyond existing Stripe billing routes.
268: - Real-time sockets; use polling where needed.
269: - SSO / SAML; keep local auth.

(End of file - total 269 lines)
